description_long: This object is a vintage computer terminal featuring a large, upright CRT monitor encased in a rectangular, dark bezel. The monitor sits atop a robust, boxy base unit that houses a mechanical keyboard with beige keys and a built-in printer or data entry slot below the keyboard. The front panel of the base includes a horizontal slot, several colored buttons, and ventilation grilles, suggesting it is designed for heavy-duty or industrial use. The monitor displays a framed document or schematic, adding to its utilitarian appearance. The entire unit is constructed from metal and plastic, with a predominantly light gray color scheme and some darker accents. Its overall design is reminiscent of early computing equipment found in control rooms or public terminals.
description: A vintage, industrial computer terminal with a large CRT monitor, mechanical keyboard, and integrated base featuring buttons and a document display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the CRT monitor, keyboard, document display, control buttons, and ventilation grilles.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the monitor's upright position and the depth of the base unit.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain, solid back of the monitor and base, with no visible controls or ports.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, emphasizing the monitor's profile and keyboard placement.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and dark-colored, likely made of plastic, with sharp corners and a matte finish, providing a strong visual frame around the display area.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is integrated into a large, boxy base unit that serves as both a stand and housing for the keyboard and controls, constructed from metal and plastic with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are located on the front panel of the base unit, below the keyboard and to the right of the printer slot, arranged in a horizontal row.
